methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Nordic Air Defence is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Karl Rosander. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 peers across the Defense & Security sector: 2 publicly traded companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient comparable datapoints. While 19 same-sector peers exist in the Defense & Security universe, filtering by stage (seed), adjacency, and valuation tier yielded fewer than the three minimum required observations. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial bias and was therefore rejected. No triangulated valuation range can be derived from this analysis given the absence of methodologically sound comparable benchmarks at this stage and within this specific subsector.

Key uncertainties constraining valuation include the absence of disclosed funding history, the early stage at which Nordic Air Defence operates, and the limited availability of seed-stage defense tech comparables. A forward valuation framework would require operational and commercial milestones (revenue, deployment traction, contract pipeline) not present in the available data. Any valuation at this juncture would rest primarily on qualitative assessment of team, technology differentiation, and market timing rather than quantitative cross-validation.
